title: Jeffries sets dual-track stance
detail: Calls Iran a sworn enemy while insisting Congress alone can authorize offensive action.

title: Operation Midnight Hammer response
detail: Jeffries argues Trump misled the country and risks a disastrous war.

title: IAEA access collapses
detail: Post-strike narrative shifts toward reconstitution risk and inspection blackout.

title: Jeffries backs Iranian protestors
detail: Internal regime brutality becomes explicit in the archive.

title: War Powers resolutions introduced
detail: Kaine/Paul in the Senate and Khanna/Massie in the House formalize constitutional pushback.

title: Pre-strike escalation warnings
detail: Jeffries warns against another failed foreign forever war as regional posture intensifies.

title: Operation Epic Fury begins
detail: U.S. and Israel strike Iran, triggering immediate claims around legality, plan failure, and risk expansion.

title: Casualties and counter-framing expand
detail: Minab strike casualties and first U.S. troop deaths intensify both moral and strategic arguments.

title: CNN and congressional pressure peak
detail: Jeffries escalates toward all-out-war framing while Democrats push justification and funding questions.

title: Briefing backlash and failed Senate vote
detail: Zero-evidence rhetoric, supplemental funding rejection, and the failed Senate War Powers vote crystallize the dispute.
